note: Legal freelance pipeline scrape: NEW REAL companies/firms hiring contract, fractional, or interim corporate/funds counsel. Sources: Indeed, ZipRecruiter, Glassdoor, LinkedIn (via Google indexed), GoInhouse, ALSP and law firm career pages, press releases. All leads scored 55+. Excludes leads already captured in 2026-05-06 legal-freelance scrape (EBTH, Cooley NY/Pease, Norm Law, MLA, AngelList, VC Lab, Reed Smith, Scale LLP, AMBART LAW, OGC). High-intent signals: explicit interim/fractional/contract engagement, remote-friendly, fund formation or corporate/contracts focus, recent (last 7 days) postings or active expansion announcements.

leads.name: Legalpeople (Interim Corporate Securities Attorney - Tech Co)
fitDetails: +10 corporate/commercial contracts/securities/governance focus (corporate securities + governance scope)
+10 counsel-level seniority (senior corporate counsel, governance work)
+10 stated comp at $80-$95/hr is market-credible for interim
+8 fund-adjacent (public-company securities, not private-funds — partial credit)
intentDetails: +15 explicit interim/contract engagement model
+15 100% remote
+10 posted within ~72 hrs (active May 2026 LinkedIn job)
+7 easy apply via LinkedIn / Legalpeople intake
signals: Active LinkedIn posting: Interim Corporate Securities Attorney for leading tech company
100% remote (US national)
Comp explicitly stated $80-$95/hr — market-credible for interim
Legalpeople specializes in interim counsel placements (Interim Counsel+ product)
Multiple parallel interim openings (Securities Counsel, Privacy, M&A) suggests pipeline of similar roles
Direct ALSP relationship — recurring placement potential
leads.source: LinkedIn Jobs (via Google), Legalpeople career page
leads.notes: ALSP placement firm with active interim corporate securities role. Strong fit for Katie's corporate/securities judgment at counsel level. Legalpeople is the buyer-of-record but the underlying client is a public/late-stage tech company — pitch angle: build relationship with Legalpeople once for repeat placements rather than chasing individual end-clients. Lower fit score (38) reflects securities-vs-private-funds gap, but high intent (47) due to explicit comp, remote, contract terms.

leads.name: Paragon Legal
leads.industry: Legal Staffing / ALSP / In-House on Demand
fitDetails: +10 corporate/commercial contracts/IP/privacy practice areas
+10 counsel-level seniority (in-house counsel placements)
+10 comp credible (market-rate ALSP placement model)
+6 fund-adjacent (commercial transactions, securities/governance work — partial credit, not primary)
intentDetails: +15 explicit interim / project-based / fractional engagement model
+15 100% remote (Paragon attorneys work remotely on client systems)
+10 active May 2026 postings on LinkedIn jobs page
+5 easy apply (web form), but staffing firm so vetting cycle is real
leads.compensationText: Market-rate ALSP placement — typically $125-$250/hr depending on practice area / seniority
signals: Active openings May 2026: ADA Compliance, Compliance Counsel-Data Security, Patent (Battery/Semiconductor), Privacy roles — all remote
Practice area coverage explicitly includes Commercial & Transactions, Corporate & Securities, Privacy, Regulatory & Compliance
Reusable apply form for attorneys without a current match — pipeline-onboarding signal
20+ year operator: stable ALSP relationship, long lead times but recurring placement potential
Paragon publishes 'In-House Counsel Job Opportunities in 2026' — actively shaping market
leads.notes: Paragon is the canonical 'fractional in-house counsel' ALSP for SF/Bay/national tech-company work. Pitch angle: register as a credentialed Paragon attorney; lean into corporate/commercial contracts and (with a stretch) fund-adjacent governance work. Lower fit (36) because their core placements are commercial-contracts and privacy, not private-funds — but very high intent (45) due to explicit fractional model, remote, recurring placements.

leads.name: Axiom Advice & Counsel (Axiom Law Fractional GC for PE/VC)
fitDetails: +15 PE/VC/high-growth focus (AA&C product purpose-built for private equity and venture-backed companies — partial fund credit)
+10 corporate/contracts/M&A (300+ corporate attorneys)
+10 counsel/GC seniority explicitly (fractional GC services)
+7 comp credible (Axiom rates 25% lower than law firms; market-aware pricing)
intentDetails: +15 explicit fractional engagement model (Fractional GC product)
+15 remote-flex network (global on-demand attorney bench)
+8 active May 2026 hiring (multi-position page, ongoing recruitment)
+5 onboarding gate is real — Axiom hires 1 in 25 applicants
leads.compensationText: Axiom-determined hourly rates; 25% below traditional law firm partner rates (typically $200-$400/hr for senior counsel placements)
signals: Axiom Advice & Counsel (AA&C) product explicitly purpose-built for PE / VC-backed companies and high-growth small businesses
Network of 300+ corporate-law attorneys with avg 18+ years experience
Active recruitment via Axiom careers page (continuous hiring)
Public press release: $100M+ cost avoidance for in-house clients in 2024 — they place a lot of senior counsel
Ongoing fractional and full-time GC arrangements (subscription pricing model)
leads.notes: Axiom's AA&C product is the closest large-scale match to Katie's profile: senior corporate/fund-adjacent counsel placed fractionally at PE/VC-backed clients. Pitch angle: Axiom AA&C application for fractional GC engagements; emphasize fund formation + corporate governance background. Slow onboarding (1-in-25), but once placed, deal flow is recurring. Score reflects strong intent fit but slightly weaker than direct law-firm fund work for fit.

leads.name: Sleep Doctor (Fractional General Counsel)
fitDetails: +0 fund/private funds (consumer health, not fund work)
+10 corporate/contracts/governance (entity, contracts, compliance, risk)
+15 GC/counsel seniority explicitly (Fractional GC role, senior legal+compliance leader)
+8 comp credible (greenhouse posting, established company, but not stated)
intentDetails: +15 explicit fractional engagement
+15 fully remote
+10 posted within last 7 days (Greenhouse active May 2026)
+7 easy apply (Greenhouse direct application)
leads.compensationText: Not stated in posting (typical fractional GC: $150-$300/hr or monthly retainer)
signals: Active Greenhouse posting: 'General Counsel (Fractional)' — May 2026
Hands-on senior legal + compliance leadership role
Digital health / consumer products / regulated healthcare intersection
Requires 10+ years legal experience — clearly a senior fractional role, not document review
Direct hiring (not via staffing firm) — better margins
Posted on multiple aggregators (Indeed, GoInhouse, Greenhouse) — committed search
leads.notes: Lower fit score because consumer health is not a fund/PE buyer profile — but very high intent (explicit fractional, remote, posted directly by company). Pitch angle: lean into Katie's commercial-contracts, regulatory/compliance, and corporate governance experience. Worth applying if Katie wants to round out fractional GC engagements outside the fund world. Direct relationship (not via ALSP).

leads.name: DLA Piper - Investment Funds Group
leads.location: Multiple US offices (NY, DC, Boston, Chicago)
fitDetails: +20 fund/private funds (dedicated Investment Funds Group with active fund formation hire)
+10 corporate/contracts/structuring (LP docs, side letters, fund formation work)
+10 counsel/associate-track seniority (1-3 yr posting; Katie can pitch as senior contract resource)
+10 comp credible (BigLaw rates, partner-level pricing)
intentDetails: +5 contract/interim — DLA Piper is hiring a permanent associate, not contract — partial credit only via overflow potential
+10 hybrid-flex (BigLaw flexible/hybrid options)
+8 posted recently (May 2026 active listing)
+5 standard recruiting funnel — slower, but documented opening
leads.compensationText: BigLaw market scale (associate $260-$435k base; contract attorney rates per arrangement)
signals: Active May 2026 posting: Investment Funds Group attorney role (1-3 yr fund formation)
DLA Piper has a global investment funds practice with consistent overflow needs
Fund formation work — direct match to Katie's ICP
Even though the listed role is associate-level, BigLaw fund formation groups routinely use contract attorneys for diligence and structuring overflow
Pitch angle: senior contract overflow capacity for fund formation projects rather than associate role
leads.notes: Fit is strong (fund formation BigLaw practice) but intent is lower (the explicit posting is associate, not contract). Real pitch is to DLA Piper's NY/DC fund-formation partners as a senior contract resource for overflow on Fund I/II launches. Score reflects fit-strong / intent-weaker; still worth including because DLA's volume of fund formation work is one of the largest in the US.

leads.name: Direct Counsel (Fund Formation & Investment Management Associate)
fitDetails: +20 fund/private funds (explicit fund formation & investment management)
+10 corporate/contracts (private fund sponsors + institutional investors)
+10 counsel-track seniority (mid-level, 4-6 yrs, but Direct Counsel also places contract resources)
+7 comp credible (BigLaw scale; recruiter places into market-rate firms)
intentDetails: +5 contract/interim — Direct Counsel does some contract placements, but the headline is associate FT — partial credit
+10 hybrid/remote-flex (some clients flex)
+10 posted recently (active Indeed listing May 2026)
+7 direct recruiter contact
leads.compensationText: Market-rate BigLaw fund formation comp (~$260-$300k base for mid-level; contract rates per project)
signals: Active mid-level fund formation associate placement (Boston preferred)
Multi-city hiring footprint (Boston, Chicago, Dallas, NY) signals network of buyer firms
Direct Counsel routinely places contract / interim resources beyond their associate-track headlines
Recruiter relationship = funnel-of-firms rather than single buyer
Active May 2026 listings
leads.notes: Recruiter rather than direct buyer — but the underlying client firms (East Coast law firms with fund formation practices) are the real buyers. Pitch angle: register with Direct Counsel for senior/contract fund formation overflow placements at multiple firms, not just the listed associate role. Stronger fit (47) but intent (32) is lower because they're hiring permanent associates by default.

leads.name: Foley Hoag LLP - Investment Advisers & Private Funds Practice
leads.industry: Law Firm / Investment Advisers & Private Funds
fitDetails: +20 fund/private funds (Investment Advisers & Private Funds practice — exact ICP match)
+10 corporate/contracts (LP docs, structuring, regulatory)
+10 counsel/senior associate seniority (industry-recognized practice group)
+7 comp credible (mid-market BigLaw rates)
intentDetails: +5 contract/interim — no explicit contract posting found, but private funds groups routinely use overflow
+5 hybrid-flex (firm offers hybrid)
+8 practice page actively features the practice as a growth area
+7 direct contact via firm careers / partners
leads.compensationText: Mid-market BigLaw rates (associate $200-$320k; contract attorney ~$100-$200/hr per arrangement)
signals: Investment Advisers & Private Funds practice featured on firm site as a core practice
Boston-based mid-size firm with fund-of-funds, hedge, PE, VC exposure
Practice covers fund formation + LP-side fund work — both sides of the table
Mid-market firm = more receptive to senior contract overflow than top-tier global firms
Active practice indicates ongoing transactional volume
leads.notes: No explicit contract posting found, but Foley Hoag's mid-market profile + active fund formation practice makes them a strong outbound target for overflow capacity. Pitch angle: senior contract resource for fund formation matters during peak filing/closing windows. Lower intent score reflects no specific posting; included because fit is strong and Foley Hoag is exactly the kind of mid-market firm that hires contract counsel for capacity.
